_comment: Hand-collected, web-sourced programs / related-services data and other findings for SD County districts. BEST-EFFORT and incomplete by nature: this is NOT government-dataset data. Each district carries source URLs + a research date. Booleans are `true` only when a source explicitly states it; unconfirmed fields are left null (never guessed). `additional_findings` is free-text for anything useful that does not map to the current schema — display decisions are deferred. A later pipeline step will fold the schema-mapped subset into profiles as a `district_web` source.
_progress: 42 SD + 16 Imperial + 19 Ventura = 77 of 240 districts (Phase 3 SoCal grind in progress)

37-67967.name: Alpine Union Elementary
37-67967.additional_findings: Special-education page exists (alpineschools.net) but no program detail retrievable. NOTE: general web search collided heavily with Alpine School District, Utah — needs a targeted fetch of the California district's page before any field is filled. K-6 district.

37-67983.name: Borrego Springs Unified
37-67983.additional_findings: Very small rural unified district. Special education runs under the Educational Services department; no autism-specific program or related-services detail published online. Likely relies on regional/SELPA placements for higher-needs students.

37-68007.name: Cardiff Elementary
37-68007.additional_findings: Member of the North Coastal Consortium for Special Education (NCCSE) SELPA. Two-school K-6 district (Cardiff, Ada Harris). In-district models: co-teaching consultation, push-in support, separate reteaching, modified curriculum. Higher-needs students served via regional programs in neighboring districts. (Search collided with Cardiff, Wales — only CA content used.)

37-68031.name: Coronado Unified
37-68031.additional_findings: Runs an Adult Transition Program for young adults with disabilities ages 18-22 (basis for transition_18_22_program = true), plus a Transition Education program and WorkAbility I. Staffs an Adult Transition Program Educational Specialist and a WorkAbility Coach. No autism-specific classroom named online.

37-68049.name: Dehesa Elementary
37-68049.additional_findings: Very small district with a Special Education page and an Extended School Services page but no published autism-specific program or related-services detail. NOTE: Dehesa authorizes large charter schools; its CDE special-ed counts are dominated by those charters (see methodology).

37-68056.name: Del Mar Union Elementary
37-68056.additional_findings: Employs Autism Behavior Specialists — registered behavior technicians (RBTs) who collect data, give direct support, and lead crisis interventions district-wide — plus behavior support interventionists. Occupational therapy provided. RBT-level behavior staffing present; a BCBA is not explicitly named. K-6 district.

37-68080.name: Encinitas Union Elementary
37-68080.additional_findings: Full continuum: Learning Resource Centers at every school plus a variety of Special Day Classes. Autism Behavior Specialists on staff (Master's-degree holders get a 10% stipend). Runs an 'Inclusion in Action' initiative. SDCs are need-based, not autism-named. K-6 district.

37-68098.name: Escondido Union
37-68098.additional_findings: K-8 district. Special-ed Preschool Diagnostic Team (from age 2y10m). Partners with Vista Hill to deliver early mental-health intervention to at-risk children ages 3-8 across 12 elementary schools, and provides school-based mental-health treatment to special-ed students. CARE Youth Project. No autism-named classroom published.

37-68106.name: Escondido Union High
37-68106.additional_findings: Member of the NISER SELPA (North Inland Special Education Region). Special Education Services department; no autism-specific classroom or transition program detail published in search results (a targeted page fetch would be needed). High school district.

37-68114.name: Fallbrook Union Elementary
37-68114.additional_findings: K-6 district, member of the North Coastal Consortium for Special Education (NCCSE) SELPA. Regional services (deaf/hard-of-hearing, severe disabilities, social-emotional) are provided across the consortium rather than in-district. Recruits Education Specialist teachers with Autism authorization. Runs a Parent Partners program.

37-68122.name: Fallbrook Union High
37-68122.additional_findings: WorkAbility 1 — pre-employment / work-readiness / transition program for special-ed students ages 14-22, one of the more established WA1 projects in the state — plus adult transition classes (basis for transition_18_22_program = true). SEAS program; a Paraprofessional III, Severely Handicapped role supports autistic, multi-handicapped, blind, deaf and orthopedically impaired students. NCCSE SELPA member.

37-68130.name: Grossmont Union High
37-68130.additional_findings: Substantial transition offering for ages 18-22: Individual Transition Programs delivered at the student's school of residence, WorkAbility I, and the Transition Partnership Project (TPP, a CDE / Dept. of Rehabilitation collaborative). Community businesses and agencies partner on independence and employment. No autism-named classroom surfaced in search (note: this district reports a comparatively high separate-setting share in the CDE data).

37-68155.name: Jamul-Dulzura Union Elementary
37-68155.additional_findings: Small K-6 district, member of the East County SELPA. Runs a developmental preschool for children ages 2-5. Speech and language therapy and inclusion in mainstream classrooms with added resources. No autism-specific program published.

37-68163.name: Julian Union Elementary
37-68163.additional_findings: Small rural K-6 district. Publishes a general 'full continuum of programs and related services per IEP' statement and ADA-accessible facilities, but no autism-specific program detail. NOTE: Julian Union authorizes Julian Charter School, so its CDE special-ed counts include charter students (see methodology).

37-68338.name: San Diego Unified
37-68338.additional_findings: Special-ed site lists: Adapted PE, Assistive Technology, Audiology, Early Childhood Special Education, OT & PT, School Psychology, Speech & Language, plus programs named 'STARS', 'PPPSS', 'Pathways to Graduation' (none confirmed as an autism classroom from the homepage — needs a deeper page). bcba_on_staff = true based on a district 'Applied Behavior Analysis Supervisor' job posting; not a roster confirmation.

37-68296.name: Poway Unified
37-68296.additional_findings: Transition Partnership Program (career exploration / post-secondary counseling, ages 16-21) and a WorkAbility program. ~4,830 students on IEPs/504 plans. Continuum: Resource Specialist Program and Special Day Classes; moderate/severe autism SDCs emphasize push-in over pullout. Speech, adapted PE, counseling, audiology offered.

37-67991.name: Cajon Valley Union
37-67991.additional_findings: Education Specialist roles require Mild/Moderate with Autism Authorization. Operates a Behavior Intervention Class. Preschool special education birth-5. Programs sited at Sevick School plus several elementary schools. K-8 district.

37-68023.name: Chula Vista Elementary
37-68023.additional_findings: Classroom staff trained in autism strategies (positive behavior supports, visual supports, prompting, discrete trial). Uses an 'Autism and Behavior Support Assistant' role. Member of the South County SELPA, whose specialists provide assistive tech, speech, OT, PT, music therapy and mental-health/behavioral services (basis for ot/pt = true). Parent Handbook in English and Spanish. K-6 district.

37-68411.name: Sweetwater Union High
37-68411.additional_findings: Dedicated Autism Support Team with TOSAs running Moderate and Moderate/Severe programs at all sites. Serves students 3-22. Transition: an employment development specialist; East Hills Academy (district-operated) serves grades 7-12, moderate/severe disabilities incl. autism, ages 13-22.

37-68452.name: Vista Unified
37-68452.additional_findings: Continuum from full general-ed integration to separate programs; serves ~3,000+ students ages 3-22. Named autism supports: autism program, sensory room, intensive language & behavior clinic, canine companions. OT, PT, speech, adapted PE, nursing, HoH/vision confirmed at California Avenue School. Member of the NCCSE SELPA.

37-73551.name: Carlsbad Unified

autism_specific_classrooms.name: ASPIRE Program (Academic and Social Pragmatic Independence for Readiness in Education)

37-73551.additional_findings: Two named autism programs: Hope Autism Program (Hope Elementary, TK-5), a structured visual language-enriched setting; and ASPIRE, targeting academic/social-pragmatic readiness. Both explicitly use ABA, Discrete Trial Training, Pivotal Response Training and visual supports. Stated goal: mainstream students into general education where appropriate.

37-68171.name: Julian Union High
37-68171.additional_findings: Very small rural high school district. Publishes only a general statement that IEP teams provide required transition services and resource-specialist support; no distinct named transition program or autism classroom.

37-68189.name: Lakeside Union Elementary
37-68189.additional_findings: Member of the East County SELPA. Pupil Services department with Special Education Program Specialists. Runs an Early Start Infant Program (birth-3) for children with significant developmental delays. No autism-specific program published.

37-68197.name: La Mesa-Spring Valley
37-68197.additional_findings: K-8 district (CDE district_type currently mislabeled 'Other'). Member of the East County SELPA. Continuum of program options incl. Special Day Classes staffed with teams for students with significant needs; has experience supporting autism. Early Start program (birth-3) located at Rancho Elementary.

37-68205.name: Lemon Grove
37-68205.additional_findings: K-8 district (CDE district_type currently mislabeled 'Other'). Birth-3 served through a SELPA-wide regional program; preschool-8 served in-district. Adapted Physical Education provided. Social workers deliver counseling, family support, social-emotional learning and mental-health services. No autism-specific program published.

37-68213.name: Mountain Empire Unified
37-68213.additional_findings: Rural unified district. A registered occupational therapist provides individual and group therapy (basis for ot_available=true). Speech & Language Program and an Adapted Physical Education program. Transition planning is handled in the IEP from age 16 (community experiences, employment objectives, independent-living skills) but no distinct named 18-22 program is published.

37-68221.name: National Elementary
37-68221.additional_findings: PreK-6 district in National City, ~4,000 students across 10 elementary sites plus a preschool center that partners with the San Diego County Office of Education for birth-3 services. Special education sits under Student Support Services; no autism-specific program detail published.

37-68304.name: Ramona City Unified
37-68304.additional_findings: Member of the North Inland Special Education Region (NISER). Runs the Lee Wilkerson Adult Transition Program, based at Ramona High, for transition-age students (basis for transition_18_22_program=true). No autism-specific classroom named.

37-68312.name: Rancho Santa Fe Elementary
37-68312.additional_findings: Small K-8 district, member of the North Coastal Consortium for Special Education (NCCSE) SELPA. Child Find covers ages 0-21. A Parent Advisory Council (established 2024) collaborates with the special-education department. No autism-specific program published.

37-68346.name: San Dieguito Union High
37-68346.additional_findings: Transition Partnership Program (cooperative with the CA Dept. of Rehabilitation) and WorkAbility (pre-employment / worksite training, ages 12-22) — basis for transition_18_22_program=true. Named supports: Learning Academy supports, Seaside Prep Program, Targeted Academic Instruction. Specialized supports explicitly address behavior, pragmatics, executive functioning and self-regulation.

37-68353.name: San Pasqual Union Elementary
37-68353.additional_findings: Single-school K-6 district. Services under Section 504 and IDEA. Notably, the district maintains and publishes records of restraint and seclusion incidents per CA Education Code — a possible future data field for the site. No autism-specific program published.

37-68361.name: Santee
37-68361.additional_findings: K-8 district (CDE district_type currently mislabeled 'Other'). Full continuum of special-education services per IEP. The CDE directory lists a 'Santee Success Program' site. Director and Coordinator of Special Education on staff. No autism-specific program detail published.

37-68379.name: San Ysidro Elementary
37-68379.additional_findings: K-6 district, member of the South County SELPA. District-reported ~582 students on IEPs/504 plans (~13.6%) — note this is lower than the CDE charter-inclusive count. Offers 'a variety of programs, services and supports' but none autism-named online.

37-68387.name: Solana Beach Elementary
37-68387.additional_findings: K-6 district. Services delivered both in the general-education environment and in separate settings per IEP. Early Childhood Special Education (ages 3-5) and a Child Development Center. A Parent Advisory Council with representatives from every school site collaborates with the department. No autism-specific program published.

37-68395.name: South Bay Union
37-68395.additional_findings: K-6 district in Imperial Beach, member of the South County SELPA. Points families to the Exceptional Family Resource Center (EFRC) for support and information. No autism-specific program detail published.

37-68403.name: Spencer Valley Elementary
37-68403.additional_findings: Tiny single-school district. Has a special-education teacher; a school annex project aims to add dedicated spaces for the special-ed teacher, school psychologist and speech pathologist. No autism-specific program published. NOTE: Spencer Valley's CDE special-ed counts are dominated by authorized charter schools (see methodology).

37-68437.name: Vallecitos Elementary
37-68437.additional_findings: Very small single-school district near Fallbrook. A Special Education department is listed among district departments but no program, staffing or related-services detail is published online.

37-73569.name: Oceanside Unified
37-73569.additional_findings: Runs an Adult Transition Program (ATP) for students ages 18-22 across academic, vocational/pre-employment, community-based and independent-living domains (basis for transition_18_22_program=true). Member of the NCCSE SELPA, which partners with CAPTAIN (California Autism Professional Training and Information Network) to promote evidence-based autism practices. District-reported ~2,344 students on IEPs/504 plans (~14.4%).

37-73791.name: San Marcos Unified
37-73791.additional_findings: Runs an Adult Transition Program (ATP) providing training and instruction toward adult programs and supported work placements, eligibility set by the IEP team (basis for transition_18_22_program=true). Special-education staff provide specialized academic and behavior support.

37-75416.name: Warner Unified
37-75416.additional_findings: Very small, remote rural unified district (Warner Springs). A Special Education Program page exists but publishes no program, staffing or related-services detail. NOTE: Warner authorizes charter schools, which influence its CDE special-ed counts (see methodology).

37-75614.name: Valley Center-Pauma Unified
37-75614.additional_findings: Special education sits under Student Support Services and serves all 13 federal disability categories including autism. The CDE directory lists a separate 'Valley Center-Pauma Adult' site, which may indicate an adult/transition program, but no 18-22 special-ed transition program is confirmed by name online.

37-76851.name: Bonsall Unified
37-76851.additional_findings: Runs an Adult Transition Program (ATP) that supports young adults with disabilities moving from high school to adult life, with a dedicated ATP teacher (basis for transition_18_22_program=true). Program continuum spans Mild/Moderate to Moderate/Severe; district frames general-education access from Pre-K through Adult Transition.

13-63073.name: Brawley Elementary
13-63073.additional_findings: Per a 2025 Imperial Valley Press article, the district is shifting to an inclusion-oriented co-teaching model for TK-8, with two additional special education teachers and two additional speech pathologists being hired to support the transition. No district-run autism-specific program is named on the public site. Part of Imperial County SELPA (CAPTAIN partner for autism evidence-based practices).

13-63081.name: Brawley Union High
13-63081.additional_findings: District publishes a Special Education page but it lists no specific program names, BCBA staffing, transition program details, or related-service ratios. Part of Imperial County SELPA. Specific autism programming was not found on the public site as of research date.

13-63099.name: Calexico Unified
13-63099.additional_findings: District serves approximately 950 students with disabilities across all eligibility categories. Programs offered: Resource Specialist Programs (pull-out model at every site), Special Day Classes for students needing more intensive support, and ICOE-run Center-Based / Community programs at the Imperial Valley Center for Exceptional Children (IVCEC) providing academic, recreational, community-awareness and vocational training (ages 3-22). District-published material explicitly references OT and PT as related services available for SDC/SH students. No autism-specific program is named — autism is listed as one IDEA category served. Part of Imperial County SELPA (CAPTAIN partner).

13-63107.name: Calipatria Unified
13-63107.additional_findings: Small rural unified district (4 schools: Fremont Primary TK-4, Grace Smith K-4, Bill E. Young Jr. Middle 5-8, Calipatria High 9-12) serving Calipatria, Niland, and Bombay Beach. District publishes a generic SELPA page but no autism-specific programs, BCBA staffing, transition program, or related-service specifics are listed publicly. Part of Imperial County SELPA.

13-63115.name: Central Union High
13-63115.additional_findings: High-school-only district in El Centro. Publishes a Special Education directory but no district-specific autism program, BCBA staffing, transition program, or related-service specifics are visible publicly. Part of Imperial County SELPA.

13-63123.name: El Centro Elementary
13-63123.additional_findings: Largest elementary district in Imperial County (12 schools in El Centro). Part of Imperial County SELPA, which partners with CAPTAIN (California Autism Professional Training and Information Network) for evidence-based autism practices and provides behavior-specialist consultation to district staff. No district-specific autism program is named on the public site.

13-63131.name: Heber Elementary
13-63131.additional_findings: Small K-8 elementary district. Publishes a generic Special Education page describing the IEP process (assessment timelines, FAPE) but no specific autism programs, BCBA staffing, transition program, or related-service specifics. Part of Imperial County SELPA.

13-63149.name: Holtville Unified
13-63149.additional_findings: Unified district. Special Education page states the district serves students 'on the autism spectrum' and 'speech and language impaired' students alongside other disability categories, but provides no specific program names, BCBA staffing, transition program, or related-service ratios. Part of Imperial County SELPA.

13-63164.name: Imperial Unified
13-63164.additional_findings: Unified district in city of Imperial. Special Education department led by Dr. Theresa Fernandez (Director). Department staff explicitly includes school psychologists, a behavior specialist, and a mental health counselor — district-level behavioral support is named, though no BCBA credential is specified publicly. No autism-specific program, transition 18-22, or related-service ratio is listed. Part of Imperial County SELPA.

13-63172.name: Magnolia Union Elementary
13-63172.additional_findings: Tiny rural elementary district north of Brawley, single school. No district-specific special education content is published publicly beyond directory information. Part of Imperial County SELPA; specialized programs (autism-specific classrooms, BCBA, OT/PT) for any individual student would be coordinated through SELPA/ICOE rather than at this district level.

13-63180.name: McCabe Union Elementary
13-63180.additional_findings: Small rural elementary district near El Centro. No district-specific special education content is published publicly. Part of Imperial County SELPA; specialized programs and related services are likely coordinated through SELPA/ICOE.

13-63198.name: Meadows Union
13-63198.additional_findings: Single-school K-8 district (~370 students). No district-specific special education content published publicly. Part of Imperial County SELPA; specialized programs would be coordinated through SELPA/ICOE.

13-63206.name: Mulberry Elementary
13-63206.additional_findings: Tiny single-school district east of Brawley. No active district website was found in public search. Part of Imperial County SELPA; any specialized program or related-service support would be coordinated through SELPA/ICOE.

13-63214.name: San Pasqual Valley Unified
13-63214.additional_findings: Small unified district in Winterhaven (eastern Imperial County, near the Arizona border). No special-education-specific content visible publicly beyond contact info. Part of Imperial County SELPA.

13-63222.name: Seeley Union Elementary
13-63222.additional_findings: Tiny single-school district in Seeley. No active district website was found in public search. Part of Imperial County SELPA; specialized programs and related services would be coordinated through SELPA/ICOE.

13-63230.name: Westmorland Union Elementary
13-63230.additional_findings: Tiny rural elementary district in Westmorland. District site exists but no special-education-specific content is published. Part of Imperial County SELPA.

56-72447.name: Briggs Elementary
56-72447.additional_findings: Tiny rural single-school elementary district near Santa Paula. Part of Ventura County SELPA; specialized programs and related services would be coordinated through SELPA/VCOE.

56-72454.name: Fillmore Unified
56-72454.additional_findings: Small unified district in northeastern Ventura County. Part of Ventura County SELPA; no district-specific autism programs, BCBA staffing, or transition 18-22 details published publicly.

56-72462.name: Hueneme Elementary
56-72462.additional_findings: K-8 district serving Port Hueneme and surrounding area. Operates a continuum of special-education placements determined by each student's IEP. District names three Special Education Program Specialists (Joy Epstein, Brian Medel, Amy Smiley) but no autism-specific program, BCBA credential, or transition program is published publicly. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-72470.name: Mesa Union Elementary
56-72470.additional_findings: Single-school K-8 district in Somis (rural Ventura County). No district-specific special-education content beyond directory info. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-72504.name: Mupu Elementary
56-72504.additional_findings: Tiny rural single-school district near Santa Paula. Part of Ventura County SELPA; specialized programs would be coordinated through SELPA/VCOE.

56-72512.name: Ocean View
56-72512.additional_findings: Small K-8 district in the Oxnard area. Part of Ventura County SELPA; no district-specific autism programs or specialized services published publicly.

56-72520.name: Ojai Unified
56-72520.additional_findings: Unified district in Ojai with 8 schools (~2,085 students). Part of Ventura County SELPA; no autism-specific program, BCBA staffing, or 18-22 transition details published publicly.

56-72538.name: Oxnard
56-72538.additional_findings: Large K-8 district in Oxnard. Provides Specialized Academic Instruction (SAI) for students with mild, moderate, and severe disabilities; autism is listed as one IDEA eligibility category. No autism-specific program names, BCBA staffing, or related-service specifics published. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-72546.name: Oxnard Union High
56-72546.additional_findings: Large high school district serving ~2,700 students with disabilities district-wide. Rio Mesa High School concentrates the district's specialized SDC programs and is the only site offering programs exclusively for students with Autism, Hearing Impairment, and Orthopedic Impairment — these are open to students from across the district. Rio Mesa also hosts Severely Handicapped, Specialized Academic Instruction, and non-enhanced Emotionally Disturbed programs, plus Adaptive PE, Speech and Language, and a full-time school psychologist. Most other students with disabilities are served at their neighborhood high school. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-72553.name: Pleasant Valley
56-72553.additional_findings: K-8 district in Camarillo (14 schools). Offers several special-education service models including Specialized Academic Instruction Courses at middle school sites, Co-Taught Classrooms at specific grade levels and sites, and a Pre-School Early Education Program (PEEP). Staff hold credentials to teach students with language delays, learning disabilities, and autism (preschool through adulthood) but no autism-specific program name, BCBA staffing, or 18-22 transition is published. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-72561.name: Rio Elementary
56-72561.additional_findings: Mid-sized K-8 district in Oxnard area. Part of Ventura County SELPA; no district-specific autism programs or specialized services published publicly.

56-72579.name: Santa Clara Elementary
56-72579.additional_findings: Tiny rural elementary district. Part of Ventura County SELPA; specialized programs and services would be coordinated through SELPA/VCOE.

56-72603.name: Simi Valley Unified
56-72603.additional_findings: Large unified district. Provides Specialized Academic Instruction (SAI) for students with mild, moderate, and severe disabilities, using the U.N.I.Q.U.E. Curriculum for alternate-curriculum students. Has a Transition Partnership Program (TPP) for older students and a special-education preschool at Justin Early Learners Academy. Speech & Language Services and 'Related Services & Therapies' navigation links exist on the district SPED page but specific staffing, autism-program names, and BCBA credentials are not detailed publicly. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-72611.name: Somis Union
56-72611.additional_findings: Tiny single-school district in Somis. Part of Ventura County SELPA; specialized programs would be coordinated through SELPA/VCOE.

56-72652.name: Ventura Unified
56-72652.additional_findings: Unified district that also serves as a multi-district provider for west-end Ventura County for two low-incidence specializations: Emotional Disturbance programs and Deaf/Hard-of-Hearing programs. Special-education department lists a dedicated Behavior Team of six Board Certified Behavior Analysts (Bailey Iannolo, Gabriela Torres, Dena Kocher, Manuel Morales, Mariah Satnick, Jamie Vaughn). Related services include Occupational Therapy, Physical Therapy, and Speech and Language. Operates a Transition Partnership Program and Workability program with a Career Development Specialist and Work-Based Learning Coordinator. Also runs an Early Intervention Center for ages 3-5 with non-categorical small-group classes serving students with Speech/Language Impairment, Autism, Intellectual Disability, and other low-incidence disabilities. A district-level 'Program Specialist Autism' contact is listed though no autism-specific classroom is named on this page. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-73759.name: Conejo Valley Unified
56-73759.additional_findings: One of the most extensively-published autism program inventories in the SoCal pilot. The district names a continuous TK-22 sequence of autism-specific programs: Be Me Preschool (ages 3-5, two campuses); Ladera STARS Academy, Banyan Elementary, Maple Elementary (TK-5 autism programs); Sequoia Middle School autism program (6-8); a LEAP (Learning Essential Academic Program) at each high school; and Conejo Oaks Academy as the post-secondary transition program (ages 18-22, located on the Conejo Valley Adult School campus, 5 days/week, 360 min/day, low ratio with paraeducators per classroom). Board Certified Behavior Analysts are explicitly part of the related-service team, working closely with special education teachers and paraeducators. Programs employ Visual supports, Social Stories, Positive Behavior Supports, and 'highly structured, visual, and language supports' as evidence-based methodologies (no specific mention of ABA or TEACCH by name). Social skills training is referenced as a strategy but not as a discrete group offering. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-73874.name: Oak Park Unified
56-73874.additional_findings: Small high-performing unified district on the LA/Ventura County border (Oak Park). Has a Pupil Services & Special Education department but no autism-specific program names, BCBA staffing, or 18-22 transition details published publicly. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-73940.name: Moorpark Unified
56-73940.additional_findings: Unified district. Provides Specialized Academic Instruction (SAI) per the IEP; Speech and Language and Occupational Therapy are listed as possible related services but no current staffing or autism-specific program is detailed publicly. Part of Ventura County SELPA.

56-76828.name: Santa Paula Unified
56-76828.additional_findings: Unified district in Santa Paula. Part of Ventura County SELPA; no district-specific autism programs, BCBA staffing, or transition 18-22 details published publicly.
